data.explanation: Habitual actions use Simple Present (“reads”).
options: She is reading every day.
She reads every day.
She has read every day.
She will read every day.

easy.question: Which sentence shows the Simple Present used for a habitual action?
The plane {leaves} at 10:15.

data.explanation: Simple Past of “go” is “went.”
options: goes
gone
went
going

easy.question: Choose the correct Simple Past form: “Yesterday he ___ to school.”
They {are playing} cricket now.

data.statement: He slept till morning

easy.question: Select the simple past verb :

data.explanation: Ongoing action uses “am/is/are + -ing”.
options: I cook dinner now.
I am cooking dinner now.
I have cooked dinner now.
I will cook dinner now.

easy.question: Which sentence uses Present Continuous correctly for an action happening now?

data.explanation: Present continuous = auxiliary “are” + “playing”.
data.statement: They are playing football in the park.

easy.question: Select the present continuous verb(s).

data.explanation: “baked” is used as past participle in passive “was baked”.
data.statement: The cake was baked yesterday.

easy.question: Pick the past participle.

data.explanation: “going to” + base verb expresses planned future.
options: I am going to the market tomorrow.
I go to the market tomorrow.
I going to the market tomorrow.
I will going to market tomorrow.

easy.question: Identify the correct “going to” future sentence:

data.explanation: “danced” is Simple Past.
data.statement: She danced at the party.

easy.question: Pick the simple past verb.
She {went} to Bengaluru last week.

data.explanation: General truths use Simple Present.
options: Water is boiling at 100°C.
Water boiling at 100°C.
Water boiled at 100°C.
Water boils at 100°C.

easy.question: Which is correct for a general truth?

data.explanation: “to study” is an infinitive used after “wants”.
data.statement: He wants to study for the exam.

easy.question: Select the infinitive form.
Water {boils} at 100°C.
I {am going} to my hometown on Saturday.

data.explanation: A specific past time (“last year”) uses Simple Past.
options: She has visited Paris last year.
She visited Paris last year.
She has been visited Paris last year.
She visit Paris last year.

easy.question: Choose the correct present perfect sentence:

data.explanation: “will” expresses future.
data.statement: She will arrive by six.

easy.question: Pick the modal used for future certainty.
He {has seen} the Taj once.

data.explanation: Negative with Present Continuous: are + not → aren’t.
options: aren’t
not are
hasn’t
don’t

easy.question: Pick the proper negative in Present Continuous: “They ___ playing.”

data.explanation: “have” + past participle forms Present Perfect.
data.statement: I have finished my homework.

easy.question: Select the correct auxiliary for present perfect:
We {played} hockey yesterday.

data.explanation: “will” + base verb creates Simple Future.
options: I will phone you later.
I phoning you later.
I have phoned you later.
I phoned you later.

easy.question: Which is Simple Future formed with “will”?

data.explanation: “The sun” is the subject noun phrase.
data.statement: The sun rises in the east.

easy.question: Pick the noun phrase acting as subject.
Tom {watches} his homework regularly.

data.explanation: “often” shows frequency, used with Simple Present.
data.statement: He often visits us.

easy.question: Select the adverb of frequency.

data.explanation: Past continuous describes background action interrupted by Simple Past.
options: I slept when the phone rang.
I am sleeping when the phone rang.
I will sleep when the phone rang.
I was sleeping when the phone rang.

easy.question: Which sentence is correct for a past continuous background action?

data.explanation: Present Perfect uses have/has + past participle.
options: do/does
have/has
will/would
am/is

easy.question: Which auxiliary pairs with Present Perfect?
Birds {fly} with their wings.

data.explanation: Present continuous used for planned future arrangement.
data.statement: She is meeting the teacher tomorrow.

easy.question: Pick the verb showing future arrangement (present continuous).
It {is raining} now.

data.explanation: Past Perfect Continuous shows duration before a past point.
options: She had been teaching for ten years when she changed schools.
She has been teaching for ten years when she changed schools.
She has been teaching for ten years when she changed schools.
She will have been teaching for ten years when she changed schools.

hard.question: Choose correct use of Past Perfect Continuous:

data.explanation: Past Perfect Continuous = had been + present participle.
data.statement: She had been working on the project for months before the launch.

hard.question: Pick the words forming Past Perfect Continuous.

data.explanation: Universal truths often remain in present even after backshift.
options: He said that water boiled at 100°C.
He said that water boils at 100°C.
He said that water will boil at 100°C.
He said that water had boiled at 100°C.

hard.question: Which sentence shows correct sequence-of-tenses with a universal truth in subordinate clause?

data.sentence: He said that he had been waiting there.

hard.question:
Direct: I have been waiting here
Reported: ?
If he {had arrived} earlier, he would have met her.

data.explanation: Modal perfect passive: modal + have been + past participle.
options: had arrived
arrived
would arrive
arrived

hard.question: Choose the correct tense for conditional past unreal: “If he ___ earlier, he would have caught the bus.”

data.explanation: modal + have been + past participle = modal perfect passive.
data.statement: The research must have been reviewed by the committee.

hard.question: Pick the modal-perfect passive phrase.
He said that he {had been living} there for ten years before moving.

data.explanation: “Would” is backshift of “will” for future-in-the-past.
options: will be
is going to be
had been
would be

hard.question: Choose the correct tense for “future in the past” arrangement: “He said he ___ leaving the next day.”

data.explanation: Third conditional uses past perfect + modal perfect.
data.statement: If she had known, she would have acted.

hard.question: Select the correct sequence-of-tenses for an if-clause (third conditional).

data.explanation: Present Perfect emphasizes relevance to present; “already” indicates present relevance.
options: I finished the work yesterday.
I will finish the work tomorrow.
I have already finished the work.
I am finishing the work.

hard.question: Which sentence needs Present Perfect (not Simple Past)?
The committee {must have been informed} before the meeting.

data.explanation: Scientific truths or established facts may keep present tense.
options: She said that she loved him.
He said that he had seen the movie.
Newton discovered that gravity makes apples fall.
They said that they would come.

hard.question: Identify the sentence where Sequence-of-Tense exception applies (no backshift required).
She {has been being taught} French since January.

data.explanation: Gerund “Swimming” acts as subject.
data.statement: Swimming daily improves health.

hard.question: Pick the gerund acting as subject.

data.explanation: Past passive continuous formation.
data.statement: The documents were being prepared when the manager arrived.

hard.question: Select the correct words for Past Passive Continuous.
They {had been discussing} the issue for hours when the boss arrived.

data.explanation: Modal perfect passive: modal + have been + past participle.
options: must have been completed
must be completed
must have completed
must have been completing

hard.question: Which is correct transformation to passive with modal perfect? “They must have completed the test.” → Passive: The test ___ .

data.explanation: “was going to” expresses intention/planned future seen from past.
data.statement: He said he was going to leave the next morning.

hard.question: Pick the words showing future-in-the-past with “going to”.
He claimed that the formula {was} correct.

data.explanation: “has been being repaired” is grammatically correct though awkward — present perfect passive continuous.
data.statement: The road has been being repaired for weeks.

hard.question: Select words forming Present Perfect Continuous passive?

data.explanation: Past continuous passive = was/were being + past participle.
options: The house has been painting when we arrived.
The house was painted when we arrived.
The house is being painted when we arrived.
The house was being painted when we arrived.

hard.question: Select the correct use of Past Passive Continuous:
The car {had been repaired} bhas been repaired, was repairing, will be repairedy the time she returned.

data.explanation: Perfect participle clause “Having finished…” shows prior action.
data.statement: Having finished the work, he left early.

hard.question: Pick the words that form a reduced relative clause (perfect participle).
By next month he {will have been working} here for five years.

data.explanation: Mathematical/universal truths typically retain present tense.
data.statement: He told us that two plus two equals four.

hard.question: Select the clause that should remain in present after reporting (universal truth).

data.explanation: Present factual/truth often remains in present tense.
options: He said that the sun rose in the east.
He said that the sun had risen in the east.
He said that the sun rises in the east.
He said that the sun will rise in the east.

hard.question: Which choice correctly keeps present tense in subordinate clause after past reporting verb because it’s still true?
The teacher insisted that the student {be} present. (subjunctive after insist)

data.explanation: The entire “that …” clause functions as noun clause object of “told”.
data.statement: She told me that she had never visited Paris before.

hard.question: Select the words forming a noun clause used in reported speech.
Having been warned, they {left} immediately.

medium.question: By the time I reached, they {had left}.

data.explanation: Present Perfect describes life experience without a specific past time.
options: I have been to Delhi three times.
I went to Delhi three times last year.
I will go to Delhi three times.
I am going to Delhi three times.

medium.question: Which sentence correctly uses Present Perfect for an experience?

data.explanation: “was painting” = Past Continuous.
data.statement: She was painting the fence when I visited.

medium.question: Pick the verb phrase in Past Continuous.

data.explanation: Past Perfect (had left) for earlier past action before another past action.
options: She left before I had arrived.
She has left before I arrived.
She will leave before I arrive.
She had left before I arrived.

medium.question: Choose the correct Past Perfect sentence:

data.explanation: Past continuous for ongoing past action, Simple Past for interruption.
options: He was reading when the lights went out.
He read when the lights went out.
He had read when the lights went out.
He is reading when the lights went out.

medium.question: Which sentence shows Past Continuous used as background with interruption?

data.explanation: “had eaten” shows action completed before “we left.”
data.statement: After he ___, we left. (choose correct earlier action)

medium.question: Select the clause that needs Past Perfect.

data.explanation: “of reading books” with gerund as object of preposition.
data.statement: He is fond of reading books.

medium.question: Pick the gerund phrase acting as object.

data.explanation: Backshift from present to past: “is” → “was”.
options: she is
she was
she will be
she has been

medium.question:
Choose correct sequence-of-tenses (reported speech):
Direct: “I am happy,” she said. Reported: She said that ___ happy.
She {has been working} here since 2019.

data.explanation: “be + going to + verb” denotes planned future.
data.statement: She is going to buy a car.

medium.question: Pick the words that form a “going to” future.

data.explanation: Present Perfect Continuous emphasizes duration up to now.
options: I have been working here for five years.
I have worked here for five years.
I worked here for five years.
I will have been working here for five years.

medium.question: Which sentence correctly uses Present Perfect Continuous?
The cake {had been baked} before the guests arrived.

data.explanation: Present perfect passive: has/have been + past participle.
data.statement: The house has been cleaned.

medium.question: Select the present perfect passive words.
He said that he {was} tired.

data.explanation: Present Perfect passive: has/have been + past participle.
options: was stolen
is stolen
has been stolen
will be stolen

medium.question: Choose the right passive: “Someone has stolen my bag.” → My bag ___ .

data.explanation: Present continuous for arranged plan.
options: I have met him this evening.
I meet him this evening.
I will meet him this evening.
I am meeting him this evening.

medium.question: Which expresses a near future plan using present continuous?
We {were playing} football when it started to rain.

data.explanation: “that he would come” is a noun clause as object of “said.”
data.statement: He said that he would come.

medium.question: Select the noun clause acting as object in reported speech.

data.explanation: “before dinner” is adverbial time phrase.
data.statement: He had finished it before dinner.

medium.question: Pick the adverbial phrase of time.
I {have seen} that movie several times.

data.explanation: Past continuous passive uses “was/were being + pp”.
data.statement: The book was being read by many students.

medium.question: Select the correct passive verb phrase.

data.explanation: Past perfect indicates action completed before another past action.
options: had finished
has finished
finished
will have finished

medium.question: Choose the correct past perfect form in this sentence: “By the time she called, I ___ dinner.”
The students {are going to present} the project tomorrow.

data.explanation: “might” is past-tense modal for possibility in reported speech.
data.statement: He said he might come later.

medium.question: Pick the modal that expresses a tentative past (reported).
She {had been studying} for hours before the exam.

data.explanation: “used to” expresses past habitual state or action.
options: I am used to live in Chennai.
I used to live in Chennai.
I use to live in Chennai.
I have used to live in Chennai.

medium.question: Which sentence shows correct use of “used to”?
My bag {was stolen} yesterday.

data.explanation: For universal truths, backshift is not required; present tense often retained.
options: He said that the earth revolved around the sun.
He said that the earth will revolve around the sun.
He said that the earth revolves around the sun.
He said that the earth has revolved around the sun.

medium.question: Which is correct sequence-of-tenses example for a universal truth?

data.explanation: Present Perfect Continuous emphasizes duration.
data.statement: They have been waiting since noon.

medium.question: Select the Present Perfect Continuous words.
He {used to} play the piano in school.
